our_argument_summary:
Refuted by Data verdict unchanged. ISS-1663 closure is a pure cosmetic fix: the boundary between the velocity-correction kernel paragraph and the formula-relabeling paragraph now uses the standard

separator that every other paragraph in this WIN uses. The patch is targeted, minimal, and surgical — no regression risk. The substantive arguments (apparatus-vs-measurement, formula-requires-Earth-diameter, v_a/r_disc collapse to globe arithmetic, Colorado Springs location, version-history-admitted circular reasoning, cross-WIN c-vs-v_a speed swap) are preserved verbatim from c5.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c5. No monitor.py / pull_data.py touches WIN-001. Both derivation forms are static HTML. The live-power dashboard row displays hardcoded Predicted=11.788 / Observed=11.7869 with 1% tolerance. ISS-1663 was a presentation-layer fix in our review prose, not a dome-side change.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Fully hardcoded - both Predicted and Observed are static text restating the 1899/1905 Tesla figure.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 1.0% live-dashboard tolerance on a value Tesla wrote down to four decimals in 1905.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c5. No monitor.py domain for WIN-001. No API call, no live fetch, no automated comparison logic. The wins.html 'predicted=observed=11.787' is static text, and the live.html row is a hardcoded dashboard tile.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c5. v_a=1.574c is numerically within 0.08% of pi*c/2=1.5708c; r_disc=20,015 km matches pi*R_earth=20,015.09 km to four decimals. Standard physics phenomenon (spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity resonance) published since Schumann (1952).
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Tesla's US Patent 787412 (1905) and Colorado Springs Notes (1899) predate the dome model by over a century. Balser & Wagner (1960) measured Schumann fundamental at 7.83 Hz decades after Tesla's death.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c5. Neither dome derivation form uses a dome-intrinsic physical parameter; both forms collapse to the same Earth-sphere arithmetic.

holes_found.recommendation: No action needed. ISS-1663 is fixed. The other c5 holes (#2 6/18/30 Hz primary-source verification, #3 lossless-vs-lossy clarification, #4 DOI verification, #5 TLDR geometric punchline) remain open as separate ISS-1664/1676/1731 (moderate), ISS-1665/1677, ISS-1666/1679, ISS-1678 (minor), ISS-1719 (moderate). Those issues were not in scope for this queue item and are tracked in the issue tracker. ISS-1675 and ISS-1732 appear to be duplicate issues for the now-closed br bug — recommend the decider mark them duplicates of ISS-1663.

stronger_arguments.description: The c5 stronger_arguments (lossy-cavity coincidence framing, Tesla's 'estimated' apparatus-design wording, direct citation of the wins.html 'predicted=observed=11.787' cell) all remain available; they were not consumed by the ISS-1663 patch. If the operator wants to convert any of them to integrated prose, an EXP would still be useful. None is required for the verdict to hold.
sources: c5 review (WIN-001.c5.json) holes #2, #3, #5 and stronger_arguments items 1, 2, 3

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c5: Tesla genuinely was reaching for an Earth-cavity resonance phenomenon when he wrote down 11.78 Hz; his round-trip-around-Earth concept is the qualitatively right framework, and his 1.572c velocity is exactly the spherical-cavity phase velocity pi*c/2 = 1.5708c.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Unchanged from c5: (a) 11.78 Hz is the patent's apparatus coherence-time spec, not an Earth-resonance prediction; (b) Tesla's 1D round-trip formula at vacuum c gives 7.48 Hz, but the actual Schumann fundamental from the lossy 3D spherical-cavity TM-mode is 7.83 Hz — different formulas with a coincidental numerical neighborhood; (c) the dome's WIN claim is 'Predicted 11.787 = Observed 11.787, exact match' presented as flat-disc geometric evidence, which the kernel of truth does not save.

advocate_mode.best_defense: ISS-1663 was cosmetic — the patch verification offers a dome defender no new ammunition. The strongest defense surface remains the c5 'kernel of truth conflation' play (you credit Tesla's framework, why isn't that crediting the dome with publishing a real Earth-cavity number?), addressed in c5 advocate_mode. Nothing in the c6 patch changes that calculus.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Same as c5: holes #3 and #5 remain the surfaces where the kernel-of-truth conflation defense gets traction. Those are tracked in ISS-1665/1677 and ISS-1666/1679 — neither blocks publication.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Unchanged from c5. ISS-1663 patch did not touch any cross-WIN argument. The c-vs-v_a speed-swap argument across WIN-001/WIN-029, the WIN-002 damping inconsistency note in detail_extra, and the WIN-062 spherical-waveguide cross-link are all preserved verbatim.

quantitative_verification.computations: Direct substring and regex inspection of data/wins.json detail_evidence field. Patch is byte-exact match to c5 hole #1 prescription.

deeper_analysis: ISS-1663 patch is a textbook minor confirmation: the decider applied the exact prescription from c5 hole #1, the byte delta matches, the boundary content is intact, no other field was touched, and no regression is detectable. The c6 review's job is to confirm closure and not introduce ceremony. Recommended action 'no_change' reflects that the WIN itself needs no further patch from this queue cycle — the open issues (1664, 1665, 1666, 1676, 1677, 1678, 1679, 1719, 1731) are separate scope and are already tracked. Two duplicate br-bug issues (ISS-1675, ISS-1732) appear to have been opened by later analyst/curmudgeon runs that did not see the ISS-1663 closure; flagging this as a tracker hygiene item for the decider. defense_survives unchanged at 2.
